skill.name: agentdb-learning-plugins
skill.description: Create AgentDB learning plugins for reinforcement learning workflows, including template selection, experience storage, training, and evaluation guidance.
skill.summary: Build AgentDB reinforcement learning plugins from templates and train them with stored agent experiences.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The high static score is mostly caused by Markdown code fences and documented examples, not hidden executable code. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest and store learning data in a local database.

medium_findings.title: External Package Execution in Workflow Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest commands. This is legitimate AgentDB usage, but it executes a package fetched from the npm ecosystem and can create local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the workflow. They appear legitimate, but npx execution of a community package is a real supply-chain and local execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Learning Data Storage
medium_findings.description: The examples configure a local AgentDB database and insert agent experience data. This can store sensitive task data if users train on private observations or prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The local database path and insertPattern examples are directly present. The sensitivity depends on user-provided training data, so the issue is contextual rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to the AgentDB GitHub repository and website. These are documentation references and do not show data exfiltration or unauthorized network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Learn More links. No evidence shows credentials, local data, or user content being sent to these endpoints.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick execution alerts are Markdown code fences around bash, TypeScript, and JSON examples. They are documentation blocks, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show fenced documentation examples. There is no Ruby source file, shell wrapper, eval behavior, or hidden execution path in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Unsupported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to description and example lines that do not contain hash functions or cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found for weak crypto usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss AgentDB capabilities and training loops, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms. This is a high-confidence false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Supply-Chain Sensitive npx Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The command examples rely on npx agentdb@latest. Users should pin trusted package versions and review package provenance before running generated commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact npx commands are present and would execute external package code. The risk is from dependency trust, not from malicious instructions in the skill.

content.value_statement: Building reinforcement learning plugins requires many choices about algorithms, data format, and training workflow. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through AgentDB templates, experience storage, training, and evaluation.
actual_capabilities: Lists AgentDB learning plugin templates and their intended use cases.
Shows CLI commands for creating and inspecting learning plugins.
Explains how to store agent experiences for training workflows.
Provides training and evaluation patterns for AgentDB adapters.
Covers techniques such as experience replay and incremental learning.
limitations: Requires Node.js and access to the AgentDB package ecosystem.
Does not verify that generated agents converge or perform safely.
Assumes users understand reinforcement learning concepts and data quality risks.
Command examples may create files or local databases in the workspace.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Learning Agent
use_cases.description: Create an AgentDB plugin from a template and choose an algorithm for a first reinforcement learning prototype.

use_cases.title: Add Training Memory
use_cases.description: Store task experiences in AgentDB so an agent can train from previous states, actions, and rewards.

use_cases.title: Compare Learning Strategies
use_cases.description: Review supported AgentDB algorithms and select a training approach for discrete, continuous, or offline learning tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an AgentDB learning plugin template for my agent. My task is [describe task], actions are [discrete or continuous], and data is [online or logged].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Plugin Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an AgentDB learning plugin for [agent goal]. Include the recommended algorithm, required experience fields, training schedule, and evaluation metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Design Training Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an AgentDB experience schema for [task domain]. Explain state, action, reward, next state, done flag, confidence, and privacy concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Learning Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AgentDB learning workflow for [task]. Suggest improvements for replay, batching, validation, overfitting control, and safe deployment.

output_examples.input: I need a plugin for a game agent with discrete actions and logged successful plays.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends Decision Transformer for offline learning from logged plays. It outlines experience fields, a training schedule, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: My agent learns slowly after every task run.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests batch training, experience replay, and a lower training frequency. It also recommends tracking loss and validation results.

output_examples.input: I want to compare Q-Learning and Actor-Critic for a control task.
output_examples.output: The skill contrasts discrete value learning with policy-gradient training. It recommends Actor-Critic when action spaces are continuous or complex.

best_practices: Pin package versions before running npx commands in production workflows.
Keep sensitive prompts, credentials, and private user data out of training experiences.
Evaluate trained behavior with held-out scenarios before deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not train on unreviewed private data without retention and privacy controls.
Do not assume a lower training loss means safer agent behavior.
Do not run generated CLI commands in trusted repositories without reviewing file changes.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps create AgentDB learning plugins and plan reinforcement learning workflows for agents.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it train a model by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands, examples, and workflow guidance for AgentDB-based training.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill includes npx commands that execute external packages and examples that store local training data.

faq.question: Can it be used with private data?
faq.answer: Yes, but users should remove secrets and personal data before storing experiences.

faq.question: Which algorithm should beginners start with?
faq.answer: Decision Transformer is presented as the recommended option for learning from logged experiences.
